quotes.text: The only thing that matters is getting to product/market fit.
The power of a network is proportional to the square of the number of participants. This is Metcalfe's law, and it creates winner-take-all dynamics.
Technology is deflationary and competitive moats in tech come from network effects, not from traditional barriers to entry.
A platform is when the economic value of everyone that uses it exceeds the value of the company that creates it.
The trouble with disruption is that everyone knows what should be disrupted, but very few know how or when.
Innovation solves problems. The bigger the problem, the bigger the opportunity.
We're looking for companies on the steep part of the S-curve of adoption.
The total addressable market for transformative technologies is often dramatically underestimated.
Competition is for losers. Monopoly is the condition of every successful business.
What important truth do very few people agree with you on?
